fonts.heading: Chivo, sans-serif
fonts.install_command: Import from Google Fonts (Chivo, IBM Plex Sans, IBM Plex Mono)

rules.h1: text-4xl sm:text-5xl font-heading font-black tracking-tight text-white
rules.h2: text-2xl sm:text-3xl font-heading font-bold tracking-tight text-white

layout.container: w-full max-w-[1920px] mx-auto px-4 sm:px-6 lg:px-8
layout.sidebar: w-64 flex-shrink-0 border-r border-white/10 h-screen sticky top-0 bg-[#050505]

panels.video_stage: col-span-1 md:col-span-8 lg:col-span-9 border border-white/10 bg-[#0A0A0A] p-1 flex flex-col relative
panels.analysis_sidebar: col-span-1 md:col-span-4 lg:col-span-3 flex flex-col gap-6

layout.spacing: Use tight gaps (gap-4, gap-6) for data correlation. Use generous padding inside empty state containers (p-12).
layout.borders: 1px solid border-white/10. Corner radius max 4px (rounded-sm) for a sharp, tactical look.

surfaces.cards: bg-[#121212] border border-white/10 rounded-sm shadow-none overflow-hidden
surfaces.glassmorphism: Not recommended for health/data/precision apps. Stick to solid dark surfaces (#121212) to prevent visual refraction of data.

buttons.primary: bg-white text-black hover:bg-gray-200 font-semibold rounded-sm px-6 py-2 transition-colors
buttons.secondary: bg-transparent text-white border border-white/20 hover:border-white/50 hover:bg-white/5 rounded-sm px-6 py-2 transition-colors
buttons.danger: bg-[#FF3333]/10 text-[#FF3333] border border-[#FF3333]/20 hover:bg-[#FF3333]/20 rounded-sm px-6 py-2 transition-colors
buttons.success: bg-[#00FF66]/10 text-[#00FF66] border border-[#00FF66]/20 hover:bg-[#00FF66]/20 rounded-sm px-6 py-2 transition-colors

components.inputs: bg-[#050505] border-white/10 text-white rounded-sm focus:border-[#00E5FF] focus:ring-1 focus:ring-[#00E5FF]

badges.offside: bg-[#FFB800]/20 text-[#FFB800] border border-[#FFB800]/30 rounded-full px-2 py-0.5 text-xs font-mono uppercase
badges.foul: bg-[#FF3333]/20 text-[#FF3333] border border-[#FF3333]/30 rounded-full px-2 py-0.5 text-xs font-mono uppercase
badges.clear: bg-[#00FF66]/20 text-[#00FF66] border border-[#00FF66]/30 rounded-full px-2 py-0.5 text-xs font-mono uppercase
badges.ai_confidence: bg-[#00E5FF]/10 text-[#00E5FF] border border-[#00E5FF]/30 rounded-sm px-2 py-1 font-mono text-sm

components.video_player: Relative container. Overlays must use absolute positioning. AI tracking boxes use border-2 border-[#00E5FF] with subtle bg-[#00E5FF]/10. Include a 'Live' or 'Playback' indicator.
components.timeline_scrubber: Horizontal track bg-white/10, active segment bg-[#00E5FF]. Markers for incidents (red/yellow dots) placed absolutely along track.

animation_and_motion.philosophy: Minimalist and immediate. Feedback must feel instantaneous.
animation_and_motion.loading: Use mono text like 'ANALYZING FRAMES...' or 'CALCULATING CONFIDENCE [|||||  ]' rather than generic spinners. Add a subtle scanning line animation over the video stage.
animation_and_motion.hover: Subtle border brightness increase (border-white/10 to border-white/40). No bouncy scales.

icons_and_assets.library: @phosphor-icons/react (Duotone or Regular) or lucide-react (already installed). Use sharp, technical icons.
icons_and_assets.style: Always set specific colors on hover. Example: text-gray-400 hover:text-white

video_placeholder.description: Stadium view to mock the video feed stage

secondary_camera.description: Close up action shot for frame analysis

team_logos.description: Mock team B logo (Chelsea locker branding)

testing_attributes.rule: All interactive and key informational elements MUST include a data-testid attribute.

instructions_to_main_agent: 1. Setup Shadcn UI with dark mode enabled. Set global backgrounds to #050505 and surfaces to #0A0A0A or #121212.
2. Override all border radii to `rounded-sm` or `rounded-none` to fit the Performance Pro archetype.
3. Use a strict grid layout: The main view is the video player (left/center) with the AI analysis feed and confidence scores on the right.
4. For the video player overlay, use absolute positioning to draw bounding boxes and trajectory lines over the image.
5. Use 'IBM Plex Mono' for all data, timecodes, and confidence percentages (e.g., 98.4%).
6. Build the navigation sidebar on the left with links for: Live VAR, Incident History, Referee Analytics, Settings.
7. Include a bottom timeline component that mimics a video scrubber with markers for specific incidents.
8. Implement Shadcn's Form, Input, and Textarea components for the manual incident upload and text description functionality.
9. Use recharts for Referee performance analytics and historical decision patterns (keep charts dark, lines neon/cyan).
10. Strictly use the provided unsplash media URLs for placeholders. No dummyimage.com placeholders allowed.
